response:
D 

The work done equals the change in gravitational potential energy, \(W = mgh\). Here, the total mass \(m\) (student plus box) is identical for both, and the vertical displacement \(h\) (to the third floor) is the same. Therefore the total mechanical work against gravity is identical for Roberta and Mary; options A and B are incorrect.

Power is the rate of doing work: \(P = W / \Delta t\). The work \(W\) is the same but the time intervals differ (\(\Delta t_R = 45\) s, \(\Delta t_M = 30\) s). Mary therefore has the larger power output (\(P_M > P_R\)), which matches option D and rules out C. (Any initial or final kinetic-energy contributions are assumed negligible because both students start and finish at rest.)
